title: High-Level British Spy Warns of Expanding Russia Threat
description: The director of Britain’s electronic surveillance agency warned that Moscow was getting more brazen, adding that estimates suggested 500,000 Russian soldiers had been killed in the Ukraine war.
author: Adam Goldman

title: Court Rejects Alabama House Map, Calling It Unfair to Black Voters
description: Alabama is likely to appeal the ruling, which stops an effort to use a new congressional map that would likely cost Democrats a majority-Black district.
author: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle

title: Time Gave Me Back What Prison Took Away
description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.
author: Marvin Wade and Evan Bode

title: Sonny Rollins, Giant of the Jazz Saxophone, Is Dead at 95
description: Even by the standards of a music that prizes individuality, he stood out, as both a musician and a personality.
author: Peter Keepnews

title: Trump Administration to Send Americans Exposed to Ebola to Kenya
description: In past outbreaks, Americans exposed to the virus were sent home to be treated in state-of-the-art facilities. The Trump administration has already flown some U.S. citizens to Europe for treatment.
author: Apoorva Mandavilli and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

title: U.S. Aims to Give Cold War Plutonium to Start-Ups for Nuclear Fuel
description: Companies say it’s a better way to dispose of the Cold War-era material — and fix a shortage of nuclear fuel. But the plan has also faced criticism from nonproliferation experts.
author: Brad Plumer

title: Trump Declares Himself in Good Health After Physical Exam at Walter Reed
description: President Trump, who will turn 80 next month, did not give details on the exam.
author: Karoun Demirjian

title: Scientists Ditched a Scary Climate Scenario. What Now?
description: While global warming is still a threat, the decision to back away from a worst-case outlook raises questions about whether some risks have been overstated.
author: Brad Plumer and Eric Niiler

title: As Trump Politicizes Justice Dept., Prosecutors Struggle With Grand Juries
description: Judges and grand juries have increasingly lost faith in the Justice Department as the president uses it to reward his friends and go after his opponents.
author: Alan Feuer

title: U.S. Carries Out Renewed Strikes in Southern Iran
description: Military officials said that the strikes targeted missile sites near a major Iranian port that threatened U.S. ships and planes.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: What Your Kid’s Report Card Isn’t Telling You
description: Grades are going up and test scores are going down.
author: Ariel Kalil and Derek Rury

title: Main Takeaways From Pope Leo’s Encyclical on A.I.
description: In his letter, the first American pontiff called for putting care for humans at the heart of technological change.
author: Ruth Graham and Elizabeth Dias

title: The Ghosts of France’s Slave Trade Will Be Silenced No More
description: Three centuries late and not a moment too soon.
author: Andrew S. Curran

title: How Democrats Can Win, According to This Nobel Economist
description: An expert in human behavior explains how Democrats can change their primary process to improve their chances in 2028.
author: Richard Thaler

title: Clarence B. Jones, Who Helped Shape ‘I Have a Dream’ Speech, Dies at 95
description: A confidant and lawyer for Martin Luther King, he was an unseen hand behind major civil rights events, including the 1963 March on Washington.
author: Robert D. McFadden

title: As A.I. Fever Rises in Silicon Valley, Pope Leo Has a Few Words
description: The American pope wants to take artificial intelligence down a notch. Is he challenging the tech companies, or will tech take over the papacy?
author: David Streitfeld

title: A Terrible Winter for Snow Heads Into a Bleak Summer of Drought
description: In the Desert and Mountain West, towns like Kearny, Ariz., are already warning residents they could soon run out of water, but rationing so far has been patchy.
author: Jack Healy, Reis Thebault and Scott Dance

title: To Get the Strait Open, Trump Had to Leave the Hardest Issues for Later
description: President Trump is hailing the agreement with Iran as groundbreaking, even as he admits it “isn’t even fully negotiated.” But the nuclear stockpile, enrichment and missiles have not been discussed.
author: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager

title: How to Be Old
description: My advice for how to keep living.
author: Roger Rosenblatt

title: A Poem for Memorial Day
description: We remember those who gave their lives in service to the United States.
author: Sam Sifton
